subject: Mathematics
title: Look at the picture and answer

sections.heading: Look at the picture and answer
sections.content:
1. Look at the picture and answer
the following:
a) Which game are the
children playing? _________
b) Who is looking from the top?___________________
c) In Scene 1, if Rani faces towards the hut, will she be able to see
who all are hiding near the hut? ___________________
Discuss.
WHERE ARE YOU HIDING?
Hide and SeekHide and Seek
Mini
Bholu
Jagat
RaniJagat, all of you
go and hide. I
will find you.
Scene 1
Scene 2
Scene 3
Scene 4
Let Us Do
Chapter
Reprint 2026-27
In Scene 4, can Mini see all the children playing the game? Discuss.
Bholu is amazed to see how this tree looks from the top.
2. Mini, Bholu, and Rani draw the same brick. Why are their drawings
of the same brick different? Discuss.
Mini’s Drawing Bholu’s Drawing Rani’s Drawing
Note for Teachers: Children may come up with different answers. Also, discuss
with them the kinds of views present in the pictures, for example, top view, side
view, and front view.
Whose drawing shows the following views?
View of the Brick drawing Name of the child
The top view
The front view
The side view
Jagat has clicked a picture of a tree from the front.
Reprint 2026-27
3. Look at the pictures and name the objects. Also write which view of
the object is given.

Name _____________________

Reprint 2026-27
4. Jagat and Rani have made different drawings of the same objects.
Match the views with the objects.
Look around you! Try to make drawings of objects, such as chairs,
tables, pencils, erasers, birthday cap, and bottle from different views.
Boxy BuldingsBoxy Buldings
Jagat and Mini are playing. They are making different buildings using
empty matchboxes and making their drawings from different views.
Now, you also collect empty matchboxes or any other empty boxes to
create different buildings and draw their top, side, and front views.
You can challenge your friends by asking them to match your drawing
with the right building.
Side view Front view Front viewTop view Front view Top view
Front view Back view Top view
Reprint 2026-27
Cat Finds JagatCat Finds Jagat
The next day, the five friends go to school feeling excited as it is an
activity day. Jagat’s pet cat follows him to school. The cat sits on the
window and tries to find Jagat.
Jagat’s cat sees him sitting on the third desk in the first row.
1. Mark Jagat’s position in the picture.
2. Describe the position of the blue bag _____________________.
3. What do you see on the middle desk of the second row?
_____________________.
4. Where is the notebook kept — the first desk in the second row or the
middle desk in the third row?
5. Draw an apple on the third desk of the second row.
Let Us Do
Reprint 2026-27
Here are the clues given by Rani to
fill the grid:
• An eraser at the top right
corner.
• A pencil in the top left corner.
• An apple in the middle of
the second row and second
column.
• A water bottle in the third row
and second column.
Note for Teachers: Let the children play the grid game in the class. They can draw
the grid on the table or on the floor. They can place different objects at different
positions of the grid.
Row 3
BHOLU’S GRID
Row 2
Row 1
Column 1 Column 2 Column 3
Come let us play.
Bholu, now you have to
draw the objects in the grid
by following my clues. Draw
the ball at the bottom left
corner.
Okay, it is the first
row and first column.
Grid GameGrid Game
Reprint 2026-27
Times-10Times-10Grid Game — Treasure HuntGrid Game — Treasure Hunt
In this game, one player has to think of an object from the grid and help
the other player locate it.
Let’s see how Jagat and Mini are playing.
Note for Teachers: How to play—There are objects placed in a 4×4 grid. The first
player helps the second player to reach the object he/she has thought of by giving
clues. The players can move up, down, left or right. No diagonal movements are
allowed. Children can play this game in pairs. Let student write/speak about the
paths: provide them with prompts/questions/fillers, such as from the starting
point, I moved ___steps left/right. Then I moved ___steps up/down and so on.
Now, you are still 2
steps away,
Mini takes 2 steps to the left and 1 step
upwards. She reaches the _____________
Mark Mini’s position on the grid.
What object did Jagat think of?
Mini takes 4 steps to the right
from the starting point.
You are 3 steps away
Is it an orange?
Yes!
Jagat, have you
thought of the object?
Reprint 2026-27
Play the game with your
partner.
From the starting point,
trace the paths to reach the
flower. How many steps are
required for each path?
Speak out or write down the
different paths followed to
reach the flower.
From the starting point,
where would you reach in
a smaller number of steps:
the mango or the orange?
START
1 2 3 4
Yes, our school has walls
made of red bricks.
Wow! isn’t it our school? So green
and beautiful.
Gyan has got a drone to show her friends. A drone
can take pictures while flying.
Circle the places or things that you see in the picture and write their
names.
Drone Around the SchoolDrone Around the School
Reprint 2026-27
Preet’s elder sister has made a sight map of the school. You can easily
locate Grade 4 on the map.
Trace the path from the Grade 4 classroom to the stage.
Playground
Principal
Office
Classrooms
Middle Wing
Service Lane
Main Road
Sports room
Medical room
Classrooms

MDM kitchen
Parking
(staff)

Entrance
Park 
Park
Grade
IV
Library
Parking
(Students)
How many routes were you able to find? ________ (You may use different
colour pencils to trace the different routes)
Which is the shortest route? How do you know?
The water delivery man has turned left from the entrance. Help him
reach MDM Kitchen by telling him the route. Write the directions below.
Rajat is not feeling well. Which way will you choose to take him to the
medical room from the library?
After the assembly in the playground, Bholu must go to the IT room and
Rani has to go to the sports room.
Trace their paths. Which way is longer?
Reprint 2026-27
Let Us Do (Project Work)
Draw a sight map to show the way from your school entrance to your
classroom and any other important places.
Reprint 2026-27

learningObjectives: Understand the key concepts of Look at the picture and answer
Identify and explain the main ideas covered in this chapter
Apply knowledge from this chapter to real-world situations
Recall important terms and definitions from Look at the picture and answer
